title: corylus mandshurica var. fargesii
intro: Corylus mandshurica var. fargesii, also known as Farges' Hazel or Farges' Korean Hazelnut, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China and Korea. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to lower blood sugar levels, and its leaves and nuts have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including diabetes, inflammation, and cardiovascular disease. The health benefits of Corylus mandshurica var. fargesii include antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, as well as the potential to improve lipid profiles and reduce blood pressure. In herbal preparations, the leaves and nuts are often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are consumed to reap the medicinal and health benefits of this plant.
